user.title: Belgian Electricity Prices
user.description: Pick your supplier and region. Energy and grid rates are fetched live from the supplier's own publication.

contract.title: Contract
contract.description: Pick your contract. The integration fetches its current rate sheet on every refresh.

meter.title: Meter type
meter.description: Single-rate meter (mono), dual-time meter (peak/off-peak), or smart meter for dynamic contracts.

dso_tariff_mode.title: Distribution tariff (Wallonia)
dso_tariff_mode.description: How your DSO bills you for the network. 'Simple' / 'Bi-horaire' match the existing meter convention. 'Tarif Impact' uses the CWaPE 3-band hour-of-day rates (PIC 17-22, MEDIUM 7-11 + 22-1, ECO 1-7 + 11-17) and requires a smart meter.

api_key.title: ENTSO-E API key
api_key.description: Required for dynamic contracts. Request one for free at transparency.entsoe.eu.

capacity.title: Capacity tariff (Flanders)
capacity.description: Pick a sensor that exposes your monthly peak in kW or use a fixed value.

solar.title: Solar panels
solar.description: Inverter capacity in kVA and the regime that applies to your installation. The Walloon compensation regime is reserved for installations certified before 2024-01-01 and stays valid until 2030-12-31; new installations and Flemish smart meters are on the injection tariff.

meters.title: Energy meters (current year cost)
meters.description: Optional. Either point at the four day/night registers from your meter (preferred - exact from the start), or at single cumulative consumption / injection sensors (the integration reads kWh from HA's recorder and splits each past day's total into day/night via the bi-hourly schedule when needed). Each side - consumption, injection - is resolved independently; you can mix one side as registers and the other as a single total. When both wirings are filled for the same side, the day/night registers win. If you've already set up HA's Energy dashboard, the cumulative fields - and the day/night registers when a utility_meter helper splits the grid source into peak/offpeak (or jour/nuit, dag/nacht, piek/dal) tariffs - are pre-selected for you. Anything pre-filled stays editable.

abort.supplier_region_unavailable: This supplier does not sell residential electricity in the selected region. Please go back and pick a different combination.
abort.already_configured: Another entry already polls the same (supplier, contract, region, DSO) tuple. Edit that entry instead, or pick a different combination.
abort.compare_no_alternative: No other compatible supplier is available in your region for this kind of contract.

error.invalid_api_key: ENTSO-E rejected this token. Double-check the value at transparency.entsoe.eu (My Account Settings -> Web API Security Token).
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach ENTSO-E to verify the token. Check your network and try again.

edit.title: Belgian Electricity Prices
edit.description: Pick your supplier and region. Energy and grid rates are fetched live from the supplier's own publication.

compare.title: Compare another supplier
compare.description: Pick a supplier to quote against. Region, DSO, meter type, peak and solar settings stay the same as your current entry, so the comparison is apples-to-apples. Only suppliers offering a contract of the same kind as yours (static vs dynamic) appear here.

compare_contract.title: Compare another supplier
compare_contract.description: Pick a contract from {supplier}. Only contracts of the same kind as yours are listed.

compare_meter.title: Compare another supplier
compare_meter.description: Meter type for the comparison. Defaults to your current entry's setting; change it to see what the bill would look like under a different billing mode (mono = single rate, bi = peak / off-peak).

compare_api_key.title: Compare another supplier
compare_api_key.description: The supplier you picked offers a dynamic contract priced against ENTSO-E day-ahead spots. Paste an ENTSO-E API token so the quote can fetch the current hour's spot for the dynamic side. The token is used only for the quote and is not saved.

compare_result.title: One-off quote
compare_result.description:
Meter type used: {meter_used}. Annual estimate uses {annual_kwh} kWh consumed ({consumption_source}). {solar_note}

**Your current entry**
  {current_supplier} - {current_contract}
  Per kWh now: {current_per_kwh} EUR/kWh
  Estimated yearly bill: {current_annual} EUR/year

**Compared with**
  {compare_supplier} - {compare_contract}
  Per kWh now: {compare_per_kwh} EUR/kWh
  Estimated yearly bill: {compare_annual} EUR/year

**Annual difference**: {delta_annual} EUR/year (positive means the alternative is more expensive).

```
{annual_chart}
```

**Year-to-date so far** ({ytd_kwh} kWh consumed, {ytd_injection_kwh} kWh injected since 1 January at each supplier's current rate):
  Current: {current_ytd} EUR
  Alternative: {compare_ytd} EUR
  Difference: {delta_ytd} EUR

```
{ytd_chart}
```

{error}

Nothing has been saved. Submit to close this dialog.

options.impact: Tarif Impact (3 bands by hour of day)

options.injection: Injection tariff (post-2024 install or smart meter)

refresh.name: Refresh
refresh.description: Force every Belgian Electricity Prices entry to re-fetch its supplier snapshot and ENTSO-E spot prices immediately, instead of waiting for the next refresh tick.

cheapest_window.name: Cheapest window
cheapest_window.description: Return the cheapest contiguous window of N hours in the upcoming price table.

duration_hours.name: Duration
duration_hours.description: Window length in hours (rounded to the nearest whole hour; the price table is hourly).

earliest_start.name: Earliest start
earliest_start.description: Optional. Truncated down to the start of the hour (14:30 still considers the 14:00 slot). Defaults to now.

latest_end.name: Latest end
latest_end.description: Optional. Defaults to the end of the cached price table.

most_expensive_window.name: Most expensive window
most_expensive_window.description: Return the most expensive contiguous window of N hours in the upcoming price table.

duration_hours.name: Duration
duration_hours.description: Window length in hours (rounded to the nearest whole hour; the price table is hourly).

earliest_start.name: Earliest start
earliest_start.description: Optional. Truncated down to the start of the hour (14:30 still considers the 14:00 slot). Defaults to now.

latest_end.name: Latest end
latest_end.description: Optional. Defaults to the end of the cached price table.

backfill_statistics.name: Backfill statistics
backfill_statistics.description: Populate the recorder's long-term statistics for this entry's price sensors and current_year_cost across a date range, so the Energy dashboard shows history that predates the entry's first live tick.

start.name: Start
start.description: First hour to backfill. Defaults to Jan 1 00:00 of the current local year.

end.name: End
end.description: First hour NOT to backfill (exclusive). Defaults to the current hour.

clear.name: Clear ENTIRE series before re-import
clear.description: Destructive: deletes every existing row of the target series (not just the requested range), then the re-import repopulates only the requested range. Use ONLY for a full-year re-run. For narrower windows, leave this off: the import upserts on (statistic_id, hour) and already overwrites the requested hours without touching anything outside the range.

not_enough_hours.message: Only {available} hours are available in the requested window; need {needed}.

duration_too_small.message: duration_hours must be at least 1 (the price table is hourly).

no_loaded_entry.message: No loaded Belgian Electricity Prices entry.

no_loaded_entry_with_id.message: No loaded Belgian Electricity Prices entry with id {entry_id}.

entry_reloading.message: The entry is reloading; try again in a moment.

price_table_empty.message: The price table is empty; refresh the entry first.

snapshot_stale.title: Tariff data for {supplier} ({contract}) is more than {days} days old
snapshot_stale.description: The integration could not refresh the tariff card from the supplier and is serving cached data older than {days} days. Last fetch error: {last_error}. Prices may no longer reflect what is actually billed. Try the be_electricity_prices.refresh service, check your network, or open an issue on GitHub if the problem persists.

extractor_failed.title: Could not parse the tariff card for {supplier} ({contract})
extractor_failed.description: The supplier extractor raised an error: {error}. The integration is still serving the last successfully parsed prices, but they may no longer match what is actually billed. This usually means the supplier changed the layout of its tariff PDF or HTML page; please open an issue on GitHub with the supplier and contract so the extractor can be updated.

entsoe_auth_failed.title: ENTSO-E rejected the API key for {supplier} ({contract})
entsoe_auth_failed.description: transparency.entsoe.eu returned 401 for the configured API key: {error}. Edit the entry's options and replace the key with a fresh token from your transparency.entsoe.eu account (My Account Settings -> Web API Security Token). Until then this entry's dynamic price sensors will go unknown.
